name: osprofiler

metadata.name: osprofiler
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# OSProfiler -- Library for cross-project profiling

```{image} https://governance.openstack.org/tc/badges/osprofiler.svg
:target: https://governance.openstack.org/tc/reference/tags/index.html
```

% Change things from this point on

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/osprofiler.svg
:alt: Latest Version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/osprofiler/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/osprofiler.svg
:alt: Downloads
:target: https://pypi.org/project/osprofiler/
```

OSProfiler provides a tiny but powerful library that is used by
most (soon to be all) OpenStack projects and their python clients. It
provides functionality to be able to generate 1 trace per request, that goes
through all involved services. This trace can then be extracted and used
to build a tree of calls which can be quite handy for a variety of
reasons (for example in isolating cross-project performance issues).

- Free software: Apache license
- Documentation: <https://docs.openstack.org/osprofiler/latest/>
- Source: <https://opendev.org/openstack/osprofiler>
- Bugs: <https://bugs.launchpad.net/osprofiler>
- Release notes: <https://docs.openstack.org/releasenotes/osprofiler>

modules.name: osprofiler.hacking.checks
documentation:
Guidelines for writing new hacking checks

 - Use only for OSProfiler specific tests. OpenStack general tests
   should be submitted to the common 'hacking' module.
 - Pick numbers in the range N3xx. Find the current test with
   the highest allocated number and then pick the next value.
 - Keep the test method code in the source file ordered based
   on the N3xx value.
 - List the new rule in the top level HACKING.rst file
 - Add test cases for each new rule to tests/unit/test_hacking.py
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); you may
not use this file except in compliance with the License. You may obtain
a copy of the License at
http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT
WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the
License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations
under the License.

functions.name: osprofiler.hacking.checks.check_assert_methods_from_mock
documentation:
Ensure that `assert_*` methods from `mock` library is used correctly

N301 - base error number
N302 - related to nonexistent "assert_called"
N303 - related to nonexistent "assert_called_once"

functions.name: osprofiler.hacking.checks.assert_true_instance
documentation:
Check for assertTrue(isinstance(a, b)) sentences

N320

functions.name: osprofiler.hacking.checks.assert_equal_type
documentation:
Check for assertEqual(type(A), B) sentences

N321

functions.name: osprofiler.hacking.checks.assert_equal_none
documentation:
Check for assertEqual(A, None) or assertEqual(None, A) sentences

N322

functions.name: osprofiler.hacking.checks.assert_true_or_false_with_in
documentation:
Check assertTrue/False(A in/not in B) with collection contents

Check for assertTrue/False(A in B), assertTrue/False(A not in B),
assertTrue/False(A in B, message) or assertTrue/False(A not in B, message)
sentences.

N323

functions.name: osprofiler.hacking.checks.assert_equal_in
documentation:
Check assertEqual(A in/not in B, True/False) with collection contents

Check for assertEqual(A in B, True/False), assertEqual(True/False, A in B),
assertEqual(A not in B, True/False) or assertEqual(True/False, A not in B)
sentences.

N324

functions.name: osprofiler.hacking.checks.check_quotes
documentation:
Check that single quotation marks are not used

N350

functions.name: osprofiler.hacking.checks.check_no_constructor_data_struct
documentation:
Check that data structs (lists, dicts) are declared using literals

N351

functions.name: osprofiler.hacking.checks.check_dict_formatting_in_string
documentation:
Check that strings do not use dict-formatting with a single replacement

N352

functions.name: osprofiler.hacking.checks.check_using_unicode
documentation:
Check crosspython unicode usage

N353

functions.name: osprofiler.hacking.checks.check_raises
documentation:
Check raises usage

N354

functions.name: osprofiler._utils.split
documentation:
Splits a comma separated text blob into its components.

Does nothing if already a list or tuple.

functions.name: osprofiler._utils.binary_encode
documentation:
Converts a string of into a binary type using given encoding.

Does nothing if text not unicode string.

functions.name: osprofiler._utils.binary_decode
documentation:
Converts a binary type into a text type using given encoding.

Does nothing if data is already unicode string.

functions.name: osprofiler._utils.generate_hmac
documentation: Generate a hmac using a known key given the provided content.

functions.name: osprofiler._utils.signed_pack
documentation: Pack and sign data with hmac_key.

functions.name: osprofiler._utils.signed_unpack
documentation:
Unpack data and check that it was signed with hmac_key.

```
param data

json string that was singed_packed.

param hmac_data

hmac data that was generated from json by hmac_key on
user side

param hmac_keys

server side hmac_keys, one of these should be the same
as user used to sign with

returns

None in case of something wrong, Object in case of everything OK.
```

functions.name: osprofiler._utils.itersubclasses
documentation: Generator over all subclasses of a given class in depth first order.

functions.name: osprofiler._utils.import_modules_from_package
documentation:
Import modules from package and append into sys.modules

:param: package - Full package name. For example: rally.deploy.engines

functions.name: osprofiler._utils.shorten_id
documentation: Convert from uuid4 to 64 bit id for OpenTracing

functions.name: osprofiler._utils.uuid_to_int128
documentation: Convert from uuid4 to 128 bit id for OpenTracing

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.elasticsearch_driver.ElasticsearchDriver.__init__
documentation: Elasticsearch driver for OSProfiler.

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.elasticsearch_driver.ElasticsearchDriver.notify
documentation:
Send notifications to Elasticsearch.

```
param info

Contains information about trace element.
In payload dict there are always 3 ids:
"base_id" - uuid that is common for all notifications
related to one trace. Used to simplify retrieving of all
trace elements from Elasticsearch.
"parent_id" - uuid of parent element in trace
"trace_id" - uuid of current element in trace
With parent_id and trace_id it's quite simple to build
tree of trace elements, which simplify analyze of trace.
```

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.elasticsearch_driver.ElasticsearchDriver.notify_error_trace
documentation: Store base_id and timestamp of error trace to a separate index.

```
and 'timestamp'

returns

List of traces, where each trace is a dictionary containing
```

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.messaging.Messaging.__init__
documentation:
Driver that uses messaging as transport for notifications

```
param connection_str

OSProfiler driver connection string,
equals to messaging://

param project

project name that will be included into notification

param service

service name that will be included into notification

param host

host name that will be included into notification

param context

oslo.messaging context

param conf

oslo.config CONF object

param transport_url

oslo.messaging transport, e.g.
rabbit://rabbit:password@devstack:5672/

param idle_timeout

how long to wait for new notifications after
the last one seen in the trace; this parameter is useful to
collect full trace of asynchronous commands, e.g. when user
runs  right after 

param kwargs

black hole for any other parameters
```

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.messaging.Messaging.notify
documentation:
Send notifications to backend via oslo.messaging notifier API.

```
param info

Contains information about trace element.
In payload dict there are always 3 ids:
"base_id" - uuid that is common for all notifications
related to one trace.
"parent_id" - uuid of parent element in trace
"trace_id" - uuid of current element in trace
With parent_id and trace_id it's quite simple to build
tree of trace elements, which simplify analyze of trace.

param context

request context that is mostly used to specify
current active user and tenant.
```

documentation: Send trace to Log Insight server.

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.loginsight.LogInsightDriver.get_report
documentation:
Retrieves and parses trace data from Log Insight.

```
param base_id

Trace base ID
```

Driver for storing trace data in VMware vRealize Log Insight.

The driver uses Log Insight ingest service to store trace data and uses
the query service to retrieve it. The minimum required Log Insight version
is 3.3.

The connection string to initialize the driver should be of the format:
loginsight://<username>:<password>@<loginsight-host>

If the username or password contains the character ':' or '@', it must be
escaped using URL encoding. For example, the connection string to connect
to Log Insight server at 10.1.2.3 using username "osprofiler" and password
"p@ssword" is: loginsight://osprofiler:p%40ssword@10.1.2.3

classes.name: osprofiler.drivers.base.Driver

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.base.Driver.notify
documentation:
This method will be called on each notifier.notify() call.

To add new drivers you should, create new subclass of this class and
implement notify method.

```
param info

Contains information about trace element.
In payload dict there are always 3 ids:
"base_id" - uuid that is common for all notifications
related to one trace. Used to simplify retrieving of all
trace elements from the backend.
"parent_id" - uuid of parent element in trace
"trace_id" - uuid of current element in trace
With parent_id and trace_id it's quite simple to build
tree of trace elements, which simplify analyze of trace.
```

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.base.Driver.get_report
documentation:
Forms and returns report composed from the stored notifications.

```
param base_id

Base id of trace elements.
```

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.base.Driver.get_name
documentation: Returns backend specific name for the driver.

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.base.Driver.list_traces
documentation:
Query all traces from the storage.

```
param fields

Set of trace fields to return. Defaults to 'base_id'
and 'timestamp'

returns

List of traces, where each trace is a dictionary containing
at least  and .
```

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.base.Driver.list_error_traces
documentation:
Query all error traces from the storage.

:return List of traces, where each trace is a dictionary containing
        `base_id` and `timestamp`.

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.base.Driver._build_tree
documentation:
Builds the tree (forest) data structure based on the list of nodes.

Tree building works in O(n\*log(n)).

```
param nodes

dict of nodes, where each node is a dictionary with fields
"parent_id", "trace_id", "info"

returns

list of top level ("root") nodes in form of dictionaries,
each containing the "info" and "children" fields, where
"children" is the list of child nodes ("children" will be
empty for leafs)
```

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.base.Driver._append_results
documentation:
Appends the notification to the dictionary of notifications.

```
param trace_id

UUID of current trace point

param parent_id

UUID of parent trace point

param name

name of operation

param project

project name

param service

service name

param host

host name or FQDN

param timestamp

Unicode-style timestamp matching the pattern
"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%f" , e.g. 2016-04-18T17:42:10.77

param raw_payload

raw notification without any filtering, with all
fields included
```

methods.name: osprofiler.drivers.base.Driver._parse_results
documentation:
Parses Driver's notifications placed by _append_results() .

:returns: full profiling report

Base Driver class.

This class provides protected common methods that
do not rely on a specific storage backend. Public methods notify() and/or
get_report(), which require using storage backend API, must be overridden
and implemented by any class derived from this class.

```
param project

project name (keystone, cinder etc.)

service name that will be profiled

hostname or host IP address that the service will be
running on.

param kwargs

other arguments for notifier creation
```

functions.name: osprofiler.web.get_trace_id_headers
documentation: Adds the trace id headers (and any hmac) into provided dictionary.

functions.name: osprofiler.web.disable
documentation:
Disable middleware.

This is the alternative way to disable middleware. It will be used to be
able to disable middleware via oslo.config.

functions.name: osprofiler.web.enable
documentation: Enable middleware.

classes.name: osprofiler.web.WsgiMiddleware

methods.name: osprofiler.web.WsgiMiddleware.__init__
documentation:
Initialize middleware with api-paste.ini arguments.

:application: wsgi app
:hmac_keys: Only trace header that was signed with one of these
            hmac keys will be processed. This limitation is
            essential, because it allows to profile OpenStack
            by only those who knows this key which helps
            avoid DDOS.
:enabled: This middleware can be turned off fully if enabled is False.
:kwargs: Other keyword arguments.
         NOTE(tovin07): Currently, this `kwargs` is not used at all.
         It's here to avoid some extra keyword arguments in local_conf
         that cause `__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument`.
WSGI Middleware that enables tracing for an application.

documentation: Disable tracing of all DB queries. Reduce a lot size of profiles.

documentation: Add tracing to all sqlalchemy calls.

documentation: Add listener that will send trace info before query is executed.

documentation:
Add listener that will send trace info after query is executed.

```
param hide_result

Boolean value to hide or show SQL result in trace.
True - hide SQL result (default).
False - show SQL result in trace.
```

documentation: Do nothing on notify().

functions.name: osprofiler.notifier.notify
documentation:
Passes the profiling info to the notifier callable.

```
param info

dictionary with profiling information
```

functions.name: osprofiler.notifier.get
documentation: Returns notifier callable.

functions.name: osprofiler.notifier.set
documentation:
Service that are going to use profiler should set callable notifier.

Callable notifier is instance of callable object, that accept exactly
one argument "info". "info" - is dictionary of values that contains
profiling information.

functions.name: osprofiler.notifier.create
documentation:
Create notifier based on specified plugin_name

```
param connection_string

connection string which specifies the storage
driver for notifier

param args

args that will be passed to the driver's __init__ method

param kwargs

kwargs that will be passed to the driver's __init__ method

returns

Callable notifier method
```

functions.name: osprofiler.profiler.init
documentation:
Init profiler instance for current thread.

You should call profiler.init() before using osprofiler.
Otherwise profiler.start() and profiler.stop() methods won't do anything.

```
param hmac_key

secret key to sign trace information.

param base_id

Used to bind all related traces.

param parent_id

Used to build tree of traces.

returns

Profiler instance
```

functions.name: osprofiler.profiler.get
documentation:
Get profiler instance.

:returns: Profiler instance or None if profiler wasn't inited.

functions.name: osprofiler.profiler.start
documentation:
Send new start notification if profiler instance is presented.

```
param name

The name of action. E.g. wsgi, rpc, db, etc..

param info

Dictionary with extra trace information. For example in wsgi
it can be url, in rpc - message or in db sql - request.
```

functions.name: osprofiler.profiler.stop
documentation: Send new stop notification if profiler instance is presented.

functions.name: osprofiler.profiler.trace
documentation:
Trace decorator for functions.

Very useful if you would like to add trace point on existing function:

\>>  @profiler.trace("my_point")
\>>  def my_func(self, some_args):
\>>      #code

```
param name

The name of action. E.g. wsgi, rpc, db, etc..

param info

Dictionary with extra trace information. For example in wsgi
it can be url, in rpc - message or in db sql - request.

param hide_args

Don't push to trace info args and kwargs. Quite useful
if you have some info in args that you wont to share,
e.g. passwords.

param hide_result

Boolean value to hide/show function result in trace.
True - hide function result (default).
False - show function result in trace.

param allow_multiple_trace

If the wrapped function has already been
traced either allow the new trace to occur
or raise a value error denoting that multiple
tracing is not allowed (by default allow).
```

functions.name: osprofiler.profiler.trace_cls
documentation:
Trace decorator for instances of class .

Very useful if you would like to add trace point on existing method:

\>>  @profiler.trace_cls("rpc")
\>>  RpcManagerClass(object):
\>>
\>>      def my_method(self, some_args):
\>>          pass
\>>
\>>      def my_method2(self, some_arg1, some_arg2, kw=None, kw2=None)
\>>          pass
\>>

```
param name

The name of action. E.g. wsgi, rpc, db, etc..

param info

Dictionary with extra trace information. For example in wsgi
it can be url, in rpc - message or in db sql - request.

param hide_args

Don't push to trace info args and kwargs. Quite useful
if you have some info in args that you wont to share,
e.g. passwords.

param hide_result

Boolean value to hide/show function result in trace.
True - hide function result (default).
False - show function result in trace.

param trace_private

Trace methods that starts with "_". It wont trace
methods that starts "__" even if it is turned on.

param trace_static_methods

Trace staticmethods. This may be prone to
issues so careful usage is recommended (this
is also why this defaults to false).

param trace_class_methods

Trace classmethods. This may be prone to
issues so careful usage is recommended (this
is also why this defaults to false).

param allow_multiple_trace

If wrapped attributes have already been
traced either allow the new trace to occur
or raise a value error denoting that multiple
tracing is not allowed (by default allow).
```

classes.name: osprofiler.profiler.TracedMeta
documentation:
Metaclass to comfortably trace all children of a specific class.

Possible usage:

```pycon
>>>  class RpcManagerClass(object, metaclass=profiler.TracedMeta):
>>>      __trace_args__ = {'name': 'rpc',
>>>                        'info': None,
>>>                        'hide_args': False,
>>>                        'hide_result': True,
>>>                        'trace_private': False}
>>>
>>>      def my_method(self, some_args):
>>>          pass
>>>
>>>      def my_method2(self, some_arg1, some_arg2, kw=None, kw2=None)
>>>          pass
```

Adding of this metaclass requires to set \_\_trace_args\_\_ attribute to the
class we want to modify. \_\_trace_args\_\_ is the dictionary with one
mandatory key included - "name", that will define name of action to be
traced - E.g. wsgi, rpc, db, etc...

methods.name: osprofiler.profiler.Trace.__init__
documentation:
With statement way to use profiler start()/stop().


>> with profiler.Trace("rpc", info={"any": "values"})
>>    some code

instead of

>> profiler.start()
>> try:
>>    your code
>> finally:
      profiler.stop()

methods.name: osprofiler.profiler._Profiler.get_shorten_id
documentation:
Return shorten id of a uuid that will be used in OpenTracing drivers

```
param uuid_id

A string of uuid that was generated by uuidutils

returns

A shorter 64-bit long id
```

methods.name: osprofiler.profiler._Profiler.get_base_id
documentation:
Return base id of a trace.

Base id is the same for all elements in one trace. It's main goal is
to be able to retrieve by one request all trace elements from storage.

methods.name: osprofiler.profiler._Profiler.get_parent_id
documentation: Returns parent trace element id.

methods.name: osprofiler.profiler._Profiler.get_id
documentation: Returns current trace element id.

methods.name: osprofiler.profiler._Profiler.start
documentation:
Start new event.

Adds new trace_id to trace stack and sends notification
to collector. With "info" and 3 ids:
base_id - to be able to retrieve all trace elements by one query
parent_id - to build tree of events (not just a list)
trace_id - current event id.

```
param name

name of trace element (db, wsgi, rpc, etc..)

param info

Dictionary with any useful information related to this
trace element. (sql request, rpc message or url...)
```

methods.name: osprofiler.profiler._Profiler.stop
documentation:
Finish latest event.

Same as a start, but instead of pushing trace_id to stack it pops it.

```
param info

Dict with useful info. It will be send in notification.
```
